- Not enough disk space. To free disk space, delete files you no longer need.
- Downloaded package is corrupt.
- Insufficient memory (RAM). Close some programs and try again.

If the problem persists contact NVIDIA consumer support.

manage3dSettings.zeroSettingsOnFilter: Configure in-game graphics settings and driver settings here

BaseProfile.description: Affects all programs settings using profile

CudaGpus.name: CUDA - GPUs
CudaGpus.description: Allows you to specify which GPUs can be used by CUDA applications, including PhysX.
CudaGpus.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Selecting a GPU to be used as a CUDA processor
  • Preventing a GPU from being used as a CUDA processor.

VerticalSync.name: Vertical Sync
VerticalSync.description: Vertical sync is a collection of settings that control how the GPU render rate interfaces to the refresh rate of a monitor. This setting is applicable only when a display is connected to NVIDIA GPU.
VerticalSync.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Off – Allows a GPU to render unconstrained by refresh rate and will scan out from multiple rendered frames in a single refresh interval. Causes screen artifacts like tearing, but has very low latency and high performance.
  • On - Limits the GPU render rate to the maximum refresh rate of your display and avoids tearing. However can have longer latency and lower performance due to render rate limits. Also can stutter if GPU render rate falls below the refresh rate.
  • Fast – GPU renders unconstrained similar to VSYNC Off, but does not tear since frames in excess of the refresh rate of the monitor are dropped. Delivers latency that is very close to VSYNC off with no tearing.
  • Adaptive - Limits the GPU render rate similar to VSYNC On, however if render rate drops below the refresh rate will allow tearing. Good compromise between latency and visual clarity
  • Adaptive (half refresh rate) - Limits the GPU render rate to ½ the max refresh rate of the monitor. Can reduce stutter for games with low FPS.

PowerManagementMode.name: Power Management Mode
PowerManagementMode.description: This feature allows you to set a preference for your graphics card’s performance level when running 3D applications.
PowerManagementMode.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Adaptive allows the graphics driver to automatically determine the proper performance state based on GPU usage. This mode helps save power when 3D applications do not demand the card’s maximum capabilities.
  • Prefer maximum performance maintains the card at its maximum performance state when 3D applications are running regardless of GPU usage, which can increase power consumption.
PowerManagementMode.typicalUsageScenariosQuadroMode:
• Prefer consistent performance maintains the GPU at a consistent performance state when 3D applications are running. It is often used to provide reproducible results during SW development and tuning.
  • NVIDIA driver-controlled mode allows the NVIDIA graphics driver to automatically determine the best settings for performance and image quality. When using features such as Mosaic, SDI, Quadro Sync, and G-Sync, the GPU will likely run in maximum power mode at all times to provide the correct image quality experience for those features.
  • Adaptive allows the graphics driver to automatically determine the proper performance state based on GPU usage. This mode helps save power when 3D applications do not demand the card’s maximum capabilities.
  • Prefer maximum performance maintains the card at its maximum performance state when 3D applications are running regardless of GPU usage, which can increase power consumption.
PowerManagementMode.typicalUsageScenariosTuring:
• Prefer consistent performance maintains the GPU at a consistent performance state when 3D applications are running. It is often used to provide reproducible results during SW development and tuning.
  • NVIDIA driver-controlled mode allows the NVIDIA graphics driver to automatically determine the best settings for performance and image quality.
  • Adaptive allows the graphics driver to automatically determine the proper performance state based on GPU usage. This mode helps save power when 3D applications do not demand the card’s maximum capabilities.
  • Prefer maximum performance maintains the card at its maximum performance state when 3D applications are running regardless of GPU usage, which can increase power consumption.

ThreadedOptimization.name: Threaded Optimization
ThreadedOptimization.description: Allows applications to take advantage of multiple CPUs.
ThreadedOptimization.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Most newer applications should benefit from the Auto-select or On settings.
  • This setting should be turned off for most older applications

EnableOverlay.name: OpenGL Overlay
EnableOverlay.description: Allows the use of OpenGL overlay planes.
EnableOverlay.typicalUsageScenarios: • A program fails to render OpenGL overlay planes

Exportedpixeltypes.name: Exported Pixel Types
Exportedpixeltypes.description: Exported Pixel Types determines whether the driver supports RGB or color indexed OpenGL overlay plane formats.
Exportedpixeltypes.typicalUsageScenarios: • A program fails to set the proper OpenGL overlay plane format

ShaderCache.name: Shader Cache Size
ShaderCache.description: Controls the maximum amount of disk space the driver may use for storing shader compiles.
ShaderCache.typicalUsageScenarios: • Shader compilation for applications can be skipped if the result of that compile can be found in the cache. This will reduce stuttering and startup time of applications.

TripleBuffering.name: Triple Buffering
TripleBuffering.description: Allows you to enable or disable triple buffering for OpenGL applications.
TripleBuffering.typicalUsageScenarios: • Turning on this setting improves performance when Vertical Sync is also turned on

ImageSharpeningToggle.name: Image Sharpening
ImageSharpeningToggle.description: This feature increases the level of sharpness, details, or clarity of your application.
ImageSharpeningToggle.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Turn Image Sharpening on to increase the sharpness of your application.
  • Use Sharpen to adjust the level of sharpness.
  • Use Ignore Film Grain to reduce over-sharpening in applications that use film grain.

BatteryBoost.name: BatteryBoost
BatteryBoost.description: Extends battery life while gaming on your laptop. BatteryBoost is an ultra-efficient mode that optimizes performance by providing just the right amount of power and efficiently selecting in-game settings to deliver excellent playability on battery power while significantly increasing battery life.

WhisperMode.name: WhisperMode

error.platformEnabled: WhisperMode settings are not accessible from NVIDIA App as accoustics are being managed by another software.

WhisperMode.description: Reduces laptop fan noise while gaming. WhisperMode makes your laptop run much quieter while gaming. It works by intelligently managing your laptop’s CPU, GPU, system temperatures, and fan speeds to deliver excellent acoustics and the best possible performance. You can also manually adjust the acoustic level to suit your gaming needs.

ImageScaling.name: Image Scaling
ImageScaling.aiHDRUpdated: Image Scaling and RTX HDR feature is updated
ImageScaling.subtitle: This setting will take effect the next time you launch a game. You can also change this in the NVIDIA overlay game filters.
ImageScaling.description: Increases frame rates with GPU upscaling and sharpening. NVIDIA Image Scaling enables spatial upscaling and sharpening for all GeForce GPUs and games. You can manually adjust the sharpness in the overlay.
ImageScaling.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Turn Image Scaling on to boost frame rates of your application.
  • Use Sharpen to adjust the level of sharpness.
  • Use Overlay Indicator to show if feature is enabled for your application or properly captured in screenshots or videos.

AIDVC.name: RTX Dynamic Vibrance
AIDVC.subtitle: This setting will take effect the next time you launch a game. You can also change this in the NVIDIA overlay game filters.
AIDVC.description: Boosts visual clarity for your games using AI. RTX Dynamic Vibrance uses AI to improve visual clarity for your games. RTX Dynamic Vibrance is content-adaptive, prevents color crushing, and works on a per-app basis. You can manually adjust your preferences in the overlay on a per-app basis.

AIHDR.name: RTX HDR
AIHDR.subtitle: This setting will take effect the next time you launch a game. You can also change this in the NVIDIA overlay game filters.
AIHDR.description: Adds HDR support for non-HDR games using AI. RTX HDR is a game filter that uses AI to add HDR support for DirectX and Vulkan games. You can manually adjust your preferences in the overlay.

error.multiMonitor: Disconnect additional monitors to use this feature
error.minDriver: Update your drivers to the latest version to use this feature
error.osAndAutoHdrAndGameHdr: Enable HDR, disable Auto-HDR from windows display settings and disable In-game HDR to use this feature.
error.sli: Disconnect additional graphic cards to use this feature
error.surround: Disable Surround configuration to use this feature
error.socFeatures: Disable DSR factors, Image Scaling to use this feature.
error.msHybridDDS: Set display and program to use NVIDIA graphics card to use this feature
error.gameDisallowed: This filter is not supported for this Game
error.gpuNotSupported: Your GPU does not support this feature
error.osInCompatible: Update to Windows 11, version 22H2 or later to use this feature

MaxFrameRate.name: Max Frame Rate
MaxFrameRate.description: Sets the maximum frame rate that the GPU will render a game or 3D application. Limiting the frame rate can be useful for extending battery life or reducing system latency in certain scenarios.
MaxFrameRate.typicalUsageScenarios: • This setting is often enabled when users are trying to conserve power or limit the frame rate slightly below the refresh rate of their G-SYNC display.

BackgroundAppMaxFrameRate.name: Background Application Max Frame Rate
BackgroundAppMaxFrameRate.description: Sets the maximum frame rate that the GPU will render a game or 3D application running in the background.
BackgroundAppMaxFrameRate.typicalUsageScenarios: User runs a game, switches to a different app for some time (not closing the game), and wants to reduce power consumption or fan noise by capping frame rate.

LowLatencyMode.name: Low Latency Mode
LowLatencyMode.description: The Latency control reduces latency by limiting the number of frames the CPU can prepare before the frames are processed by the GPU.
LowLatencyMode.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Select Off to prioritize render throughput by allowing games to queue frames.
  • Select On to prioritize latency by limiting queued frames to 1.
  • Select Ultra to prioritize latency by fully minimizing queued frames. In addition, this mode minimizes VSYNC latency when both VSYNC and G - SYNC are enabled.
LowLatencyMode.FGXDependentErrorMessage: Disable Smooth Motion to configure this feature

VirtualRealityVrss.name: Virtual Reality – Variable Rate Super Sampling
VirtualRealityVrss.description: This Variable Rate Super Sampling option targets image quality improvement by applying super sampling selectively on the central region of a frame where it matters most for Virtual Reality headsets. As a pre-requisite, the application needs to be profiled by NVIDIA and have MSAA enabled. The maximum super sampling factor that can get applied is limited by the level of MSAA used in the application. The option is available on NVIDIA Turing GPU architecture.
VirtualRealityVrss.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Select ‘Adaptive’ to apply super sampling to central region of a frame only when there is GPU headroom available. Size of central region varies based on amount of GPU headroom available.
  • Select ‘Always On’ to apply super sampling to the fixed size central region of a frame. This mode does not consider GPU head room availability and might result in frame drops.
  • Select ‘Off’ to disable this feature. This is the default option.

VirtualRealityPreRenderedFrames.name: Virtual Reality Pre-Rendered Frames
VirtualRealityPreRenderedFrames.description: Virtual Reality Pre-Rendered Frames limits the number of frames the CPU can prepare before the frames are processed by the GPU. Lower latency is preferable for virtual reality headsets.
VirtualRealityPreRenderedFrames.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Increasing this value may improve performance at the cost of increased latency
  • This setting does not apply to SLI

MaxqDynamicBoost.name: Dynamic Boost
MaxqDynamicBoost.description: Dynamic Boost maximizes system performance by dynamically shifting power between the GPU and CPU.
MaxqDynamicBoost.typicalUsageScenarios:
• On: The system will determine and set optimal GPU and CPU power levels based on usage.
  • Off: The GPU and CPU will operate at their default power levels. Performance could be reduced.

MonitorTechnology.name: Monitor Technology
MonitorTechnology.description: Monitor Technology selects the technique used to control the refresh policy of an attached monitor.
MonitorTechnology.typicalUsageScenarios:
• G-SYNC, G-SYNC Compatible – Synchronizes monitor refresh rate to GPUs render rate. Best for demanding games with highly variable FPS.
  • ULMB - Uses back light pulsing at a fixed refresh rate to minimize blur. Best for games with consistently high FPS.
  • Fixed Refresh – Traditional Fixed refresh rate monitor technology.

PowerManagementPanel.name: Power Management - Panel
PowerManagementPanel.description: This feature extends battery life by having the panel refresh the image on the screen and allowing the GPU to enter a low power state. This is enabled on battery only. This feature requires G-SYNC to be enabled.

OpenGLRenderingGPU.name: OpenGL Rendering GPU
OpenGLRenderingGPU.description: Select the GPU to be used by OpenGL applications. Choosing a GPU that is part of an SLI or Mosaic group will effectively select every GPU in that group.
OpenGLRenderingGPU.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Choosing the more powerful GPU to render an application
  • Rendering different applications on different GPUs

AntialiasingGammaCorrection.name: Antialiasing - Gamma Correction
AntialiasingGammaCorrection.description: Antialiasing - Gamma Correction enables gamma correction for antialiasing.
AntialiasingGammaCorrection.typicalUsageScenarios: • Select On to improve the quality and color of 3D images

StereoEnable.name: Stereo - Enable
StereoEnable.typicalUsageScenarios: • Turn on this option only if it is necessary; some applications automatically choose a stereo format while other applications may not function properly in a stereo pixel format

StereoSwapEye.name: Stereo - Swap Eyes
StereoSwapEye.description: Select this option to exchange the left and right images.
StereoSwapEye.typicalUsageScenarios:
• You cannot view a stereo effect or the effect does not appear correctly
  • In general, you may need to enable this option only on vertical interlace displays and in passive mode

SLIMultiGPURenderingMode.name: SLI & Multi View Mode
SLIMultiGPURenderingMode.description: Determines the rendering mode used in multi-GPU mode.
SLIMultiGPURenderingMode.typicalUsageScenarios: • You would like a different performance setting for your programs

AntialiasingMode.name: Antialiasing - Mode
AntialiasingMode.description: Antialiasing - Mode allows you to determine how antialiasing is applied in your 3D applications.
AntialiasingMode.typicalUsageScenarios:
• If you want to control the antialiasing settings from within the program, use the Application-controlled setting
  • Enhancing the application setting can provide higher quality and greater reliability for applications with built-in antialiasing support. You must set any antialiasing level within the application for this mode to work with the NVIDIA App Antialiasing setting
  • Use Override if the application does not have built-in antialiasing settings or if the application does not support antialiasing when HDR rendering is enabled

AntialiasingSetting.name: Antialiasing - Setting
AntialiasingSetting.description: Antialiasing is a technique used to minimize the “stair step” effect sometimes seen along the edges of 3D objects.
AntialiasingSetting.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Higher values provide a smoother image, though you may experience a decrease in performance
  • Application-controlled is recommended for programs with built-in antialiasing settings

StereoSwapMode.name: Stereo – Swap Mode
StereoSwapMode.description: Allows selection of “per eye” or “per-eye pair” modes for swapping stereo buffers.
StereoSwapMode.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Application-controlled: Stereo swap mode settings are controlled from within the program
  • Per eye: The driver swaps each eye as it is ready
  • Per-eye pair: The driver waits for both eyes to complete rendering before swapping

StereoDisplayMode.name: Stereo - Display Mode

values.NVAPPAPI_VALUE_STEREO_DISPLAY_NV_CLONE_D: Clone mode (with NVIDIA 3D Vision using external DIN signal)
values.NVAPPAPI_VALUE_STEREO_DISPLAY_3D_DLP_INV: 3D DLP display INV mode (with NVIDIA 3D Vision)

StereoDisplayMode.description: This setting allows you to choose the appropriate display mode for shutter glasses, stereo displays, and other hardware. Refer to the hardware documentation to determine which mode to use.

AntialiasingTransparency.name: Antialiasing - Transparency
AntialiasingTransparency.description: Allows you to minimize the visible aliasing on the edges of images with transparent textures.
AntialiasingTransparency.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Multisampling provides superior performance
  • Supersampling provides superior quality

DSRFactor.name: DSR - Factors
DSRFactor.description: Dynamic Super Resolution (DSR) produces smoother images by rendering a game at a high resolution then downscaling it to the native resolution of the display using advanced filtering. Dynamic Super Resolution can be applied to any game and can be used with other antialiasing settings to improve image quality.
DSRFactor.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Improve the quality of antialiasing on games that do not have in-game antialiasing settings or for games where in-game antialiasing is insufficient.
  • Enjoy better image quality for older games.

DSRSmoothness.name: DSR Smoothness (%)
DSRSmoothness.description: Use the slider to control smoothness versus sharpness of the downscaled image.
DSRSmoothness.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Lower value produces a sharper but more aliased image.
  • Higher value produces a smoother but blurrier image.

DLSSSR.name: DLSS Override - Super Resolution Mode
DLSSSR.description: Allows you to enable DLSS Super Resolution modes not supported in certain DLSS games or apps
DLSSSR.typicalUsageScenarios: Enabling DLAA or Ultra Performance mode
DLSSSR.notes: Latest Super Resolution model will be automatically applied while using this setting, you can change this in the DLSS Override - Model Presets setting

DLSSMF.name: DLSS Override - Frame Generation Mode
DLSSMF.description: Allows you to override DLSS Multi Frame Generation for games or apps that have integrated DLSS Frame Generation
DLSSMF.notes: Latest Frame Generation model will be automatically applied while using this setting
DLSSMF.dependentSettingNotes: Avoid using Smooth Motion while using DLSS Override - Frame Generation

FGX.name: Smooth Motion
FGX.description: Uses AI to deliver smoother gameplay by inferring an additional frame between two rendered frames
FGX.typicalUsageScenarios: Turning on Smooth Motion for games that don’t support DLSS

DLSSModels.name: DLSS Override - Model Presets

values.same: Use same settings for all DLSS technologies
values.custom: Use different settings for each DLSS technology

DLSSModels.description: Allows you to update DLSS games and apps to use the latest DLSS models
DLSSModels.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Updating to the latest DLSS models for your game or application
  • Changing to a different DLSS model

AutoDisplaySwitching.name: Automatic Display Switching
AutoDisplaySwitching.description: Automatic Display Switching for applications that are not pre-qualified.
AutoDisplaySwitching.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Off - Automatic Display Switching is enabled only on top applications that have been validated to work seamlessly with NVIDIA Advanced Optimus Display Switching technology.
  • On - Automatic Display Switching is enabled for all apps that render on the NVIDIA GPU.

OpenGlVulkanPresentMethod.name: Vulkan/OpenGL Present Method
OpenGlVulkanPresentMethod.description: The Vulkan and OpenGL API present mechanism can use an OS/API native method or layer the functionality on top of a driver created DXGI swapchain. The DXGI layered method is compatible with additional features such as VK_KHR_display and Multiplane overlay. The layered method can add minor memory and performance overhead. In some situations, only one of the methods is possible.
OpenGlVulkanPresentMethod.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Set to “Auto” for the driver recommended behavior.
  • Set to “Prefer layered on DXGI Swapchain” for improved compatibility with Vulkan Timeline Semaphores and VK_KHR_display style swapchains.
  • Set to “Prefer native” to minimize memory overhead.

OpenGlGdiCompatibility.name: OpenGL GDI Compatibility
OpenGlGdiCompatibility.description: This feature allows you to set a preference for performance and power optimizations on OpenGL windows. These optimizations can provide substantial benefit on notebooks PCs but may in rare cases interfere with applications that mix OpenGL and GDI rendering. Set this to indicate preference between performance and compatibility.
OpenGlGdiCompatibility.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Set to “Auto” for the driver recommended behavior.
  • Set to “Prefer performance” to optimize OpenGL windows for performance and reduced power usage.Compatibility with GDI rendering may be reduced for some applications.
  • Set to “Prefer compatible” to maximize compatibility with applications that mix GDI and OpenGL rendering.

AnisotropicFiltering.name: Anisotropic Filtering
AnisotropicFiltering.description: Anisotropic texture filtering affects the crispness of textures.
AnisotropicFiltering.typicalUsageScenarios:
• The image will be less blurred at higher settings, however, you may experience a decrease in performance.
  • If the application has its own anisotropic filtering controls, it is recommended to use the application-controlled setting.

TextureFilteringNegativeMethod.name: Texture Filtering - Negative LOD Bias
TextureFilteringNegativeMethod.description: Some applications use negative LOD bias to sharpen texture filtering. This sharpens the stationary image but introduces aliasing when the scene is in motion.
TextureFilteringNegativeMethod.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Set to Allow for higher performance.
  • Set to Clamp when anisotropic filtering is enabled for better image quality.

TextureFilteringTrilinear.name: Texture Filtering - Trilinear Optimization
TextureFilteringTrilinear.description: Trilinear optimization improves texture filtering performance by allowing bilinear filtering on textures in parts of the scene where trilinear filtering is not necessary. This setting only affects DirectX programs
TextureFilteringTrilinear.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Select On for higher performance with minimal loss in image quality.
  • Select Off for best image quality.

TexturefilteringAnisotropicSampleOpt.name: Texture Filtering - Anisotropic Sample Optimization
TexturefilteringAnisotropicSampleOpt.description: Anisotropic sample optimization limits the number of anisotropic samples based on texel size. This setting only affects DirectX programs.
TexturefilteringAnisotropicSampleOpt.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Select On for higher performance with minimal loss in image quality.
  • Select Off if you see shimmering on objects.

TextureFilteringQuality.name: Texture Filtering - Quality
TextureFilteringQuality.description: This setting allows you to simply decide if you would prefer performance, quality, or a balance between the two. The NVIDIA App will make all of the appropriate texture filtering adjustiments based on your preference.
TextureFilteringQuality.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Texture filtering quality allows you to easily set an overall preference for how your graphics card will render content.
  • Selecting High quality will turn off all the texture filtering optimization in order to provide the highest visual quality.

AntialiasingFXAA.name: Antialiasing - FXAA
AntialiasingFXAA.description: FXAA is a fast shader-based post-processing technique that can be applied to any program, including those which do not support other forms of hardware-based antialiasing. FXAA can be used in conjunction with other antialiasing settings to improve overall image quality. Note that enabling this setting globally may affect all programs rendererd on the GPU, including video players and Windows desktop.
AntialiasingFXAA.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Turn FXAA on to improve image quality with a lesser performance impact than other antialiasing settings.
  • Turn FXAA off if you notice artifacts or dithering around edges of objects, particularly around text.

MultiFrameSampleAA.name: Multi-Frame Sampled AA (MFAA)
MultiFrameSampleAA.description: Multi-Frame Sampled AA works by re-programming coverage sample locations across pixels within the same frame, as well as across frames. This has the effect of making the overall sample pattern irregular which eliminates aliasing artifacts.

PhysX.name: PhysX
PhysX.importantInfo: Only 64-bit applications will have PhysX GPU support on {{gpuName}}.
PhysX.descriptionWithGpu:
Selecting a GPU allows an increase in PhysX processing and may improve overall performance in games or programs that support PhysX. Choose Auto to let NVIDIA use the best processor for PhysX.
Only 64-bit applications will have PhysX GPU support on {{gpuName}}.
PhysX.description: Selecting a GPU allows an increase in PhysX processing and may improve overall performance in games or programs that support PhysX. Choose Auto to let NVIDIA use the best processor for PhysX.
PhysX.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Playing 3D games that support PhysX
  • Using 3D programs that support PhysX

manage3dSettings.importantInfoForGlobalOnlyFeatures: This is a system-wide setting and applies to all programs. To configure it, visit the global settings page.

eula.textOne: We promise to protect your privacy.
eula.textTwo: The NVIDIA App requires data to configure your GPU and application settings for the best gaming, content creation, and design experience. We need to know your PC’s hardware and software configuration, installed games and creative/design apps, game and app settings and performance data, usage metrics of the NVIDIA App, and geographical region.
eula.textThree: We use this data to optimize your application settings, deliver software and content updates, improve application features, redeem codes for free games, and comply with local privacy laws.
eula.textFour: Our privacy policy explains what data we collect, what we do with it, and the legal basis for these actions. We never sell personal data.
eula.textFive: By installing NVIDIA App, you agree to the following:

debugMode.title: Debug Mode
debugMode.description: Removes all currently applied overclocking (user provided and/or factory provided) from the GPU until system restart

eccSettings.title: ECC State
eccSettings.description: Allows to change the Error Correction Code state (ECC). Setting will be applied on system restart.
eccSettings.configureDescription: Enable or disable ECC for each GPU. ECC helps detect and correct memory errors but may impact performance.

infoDialog.description: ECC (Error Correcting Code) is a feature that lets you change the ECC state for GPUs. Setting will be applied on system restart. By default ECC mode is Off.

configureDialog.restartPending: Restart pending. Setting will be applied on system restart.

perfCounters.title: Manage GPU Performance Counters
perfCounters.description: GPU performance counters are used by NVIDIA GPU profiling tools such as NVIDIA Nsight. These tools enable developers to debug, profile and develop software for NVIDIA GPUs. Display may flicker briefly when changing settings.

eccSettings.title: ECC Settings
eccSettings.description: Enable ECC memory for the selected GPU

configureDialog.title: Configure ECC Settings
configureDialog.description: Enable ECC memory for the selected GPU

multipleGpusAppManagement.title: Multi-GPU App Management
multipleGpusAppManagement.multipleGpusDetectedDescription: Manage your GPUs for optimal AI workloads.

display.title: Displays
display.resolutionAndRotationHeader: Adjust settings for the selected display
display.allowGSYNCSettings: Allow G-SYNC settings for the selected G-SYNC Compatible display
display.displaysGsyncPanelTitleForVRR: Allow G-SYNC settings for the selected Variable Refresh Rate display
display.nonGSYNCCompatible: Selected display is not validated as G-SYNC compatible
display.gSYNCHeader: G-SYNC gives smooth, tear-free immersion and the highest refresh rates for unbeatable performance.
display.gSYNCEnabledGlobally: Settings are enabled globally for the selected G-SYNC display
display.gSYNCDisabledGlobally: Settings are disabled globally for the selected G-SYNC display
display.gsyncInfo: Features an NVIDIA G-SYNC processor to deliver an amazing experience with no tearing, stuttering, or input lag. Enthusiasts and pro-level gamers can count on full variable refresh rate (VRR) range and variable overdrive for pristine image and outstanding gameplay.
display.overrideTheScalingMode: Override the scaling mode set by games and programs
display.adjustOutputColorHeaderText: Adjust color settings for the selected display
display.globalGsyncCollapseHeader: Adjust global settings across all displays
display.hdcpDiscription: High-Bandwidth Digital Content Protection (HDCP) is a technology designed to protect high-definition content.
display.hdcpStatusAbortKsvSignatureError: Our repeater does not support HDCP. Contact the repeater manufacturer for details.
display.hdcpStatusAbortKsvLengthError: Your repeater has too many displays attached. Disconnect some of the displays from the repeater.
display.hdcpStatusAbortTopologyError: Your system has too many repeaters connected to it. Disconnect some of the repeaters.
display.hdcpStatusAbortRepeaterNotReadyError: Your repeater is not yet ready. Check the HDCP status again after a while.
display.hdcpStatusAbortHopLimitExceedError: HDCP is not supported on configurations with too many connections in a series.
display.hdcpStatusAbortDeviceRevokedError: HDCP is not supported on any display connected to a device that is no longer HDCP-compliant.
display.hdcpStatusAbortDeviceInvalidError: HDCP is not supported on any display connected through a device that is not HDCP-compliant.
display.restoreDisplay: Restore factory defaults for the selected display {{ displayName }}?
display.restoreDisplayWithoutDisplayName: Restore factory defaults for the selected display?
display.restoreGlobalGsync: Restore global settings to factory defaults?
display.globalGsyncInfoDialogDescription:
• G-SYNC, G-SYNC Compatible allows your enabled monitor to stay in sync with your game's render rate, increasing smoothness and reducing latency.
  • Fullscreen G-SYNC applies to fullscreen applications and applications optimized to render without the use of the desktop compositor.
  • Windowed G-SYNC extends compatibility to applications that render with the desktop compositor.
  • As a prerequisite for windowed G-SYNC, an application needs to be profiled by NVIDIA or manually through the Graphics page.
display.globalGsyncInfoDialogTypicalUsage: Most useful for games that have variable render rates below the peak refresh rate of your monitor.

advanceOptimus.displayModeInfoDialogDescription:
• Auto - The display is switched dynamically on launch of allow-listed apps.
  • Optimus - The display is switched to the Integrated Graphics when this is selected.
  • NVIDIA GPU - The display is switched to the NVIDIA GPU when this is selected.
advanceOptimus.switchedToNvidiaGpu: Display is switched to the NVIDIA GPU
advanceOptimus.switchedToIntegratedGraphics: Display is switched to Integrated Graphics
advanceOptimus.crashNote: Changing Display Mode to Integrated Graphics/NVIDIA GPU might cause certain applications to crash
advanceOptimus.importantInfoForDisable: Choose 'Auto' under Display Mode settings on the Displays page to enable this feature.
advanceOptimus.displaySettingsNotAvailable: Laptop display settings are unavailable due to external monitors, DC mode, or an initialization error (reboot required).
advanceOptimus.noApplicationsBlockingDisplayMode: No applications blocking Display Mode from changing
advanceOptimus.blockingAppAdditionalInfoText: Some applications are preventing the Display Mode change. Close them to continue.

scalingPanel.expandPanelSummary: Choose your scaling mode and scaling device
scalingPanel.scalingImportantInfo: Scaling is not available because Image Scaling is enabled on the Graphics page.

colorAccuracyModeInfo.description:
• Reference - The rendered pixels only go through standard color space conversions (e.g. to display sRGB content on HDR10 display) and do not undergo any color transformation related to custom LUT (except legacy DICOM gamma ramp), Display Color Transform (DCT).
  • Accurate - When CSC or custom gamma ramp via NvAPI, or a DCT with Microsoft Hardware Calibration (MHC) ICC profile is applied, the system is in ‘Calibrated’ mode.
  • Enhanced - When user performs any color adjustments via NVIDIA App or if DCT is on, but no MHC ICC color profile is associated with it, the system is said to be in ‘Enhanced’ mode.

outputColorSettingsInfo.description:
Select “Default” if you want to keep the colors which have been set by the operating system.
Select “NVIDIA” to use the color settings specified in the NVIDIA App.
  • Desktop color depth - Set the number of available colors on the Windows desktop. The highest quality enables you to view millions of colors for Windows desktop and is recommended.
  • Output color format - Allows you to select the color format, also known as color space that looks best on your TV.
  • Output color depth - Allows you to select color depth (in bits per component, bpc) of the color data GPU sends to the display.
  • Output dynamic range - Allows the user to select the dynamic range (16 to 235 or 0-255 for 8bpc) of the output which can preserve shadow and highlight details in the imagery being viewed.
outputColorSettingsInfo.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Desktop color depth - Viewing more photo-realistic images, desktop publishing or graphics illustration.
  • Output color format - Color saturation appears to be incorrect. Black does not appear as pure black. Since they reduce the color data in the images YCbCr 4:2:2 or 4:2:0, if supported by your display, can help you to display at higher refresh rates compared to RGB or YCbCr 4:4:4
  • Output color depth - Applications need to be written to support more than 8bpc output, setting a higher value here will not improve the image of an application that does not support more than 8bpc. Using higher output color depth can help you to display colors with higher quality, especially for smooth color transitions and gradients.
  • Output dynamic range - The Limited (16-235) setting is commonly used by many televisions. The full (0-255) setting may allow more detail in the dark and white areas of some content.

colorChannelInfo.description:
• Color channel - Allows you to select which colors are affected by your color setting adjustments. This can be a single color (red, green or blue) or all colors.
  • Brightness - The lightness or darkness of your display. A lower brightness level will make your desktop appear darker; a higher level will make your desktop lighter.
  • Contrast - The difference between the lightest and darkest parts of the desktop.
  • Gamma - The brightness of mid-tones without affecting shadows and highlights. Very high and very low brightness and contrast values can limit the gamma range.
colorChannelInfo.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Color channel- Your display has an incorrect hue, it can be corrected by one of the three colors. You can change the hue and color mix of the display.
  • Brightness - The desktop appears to be too light or too dark.
  • Contrast - The desktop appears gray or washed out.
  • Gamma - Skin color does not look correct. Colors on your photo do not look natural.

contentTypeInfo.description: Determines the content type reported to the display. Some displays may apply their own image processing and filtering depending on the content type. In Auto-select (recommended) mode, the graphics driver dynamically selects the content type based on usage. You can select a specific content type to see if it improves image quality.
contentTypeInfo.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Desktop programs and text appear blurry.
  • Colors do not appear quite right.

scalingModeInfo.description:
• Aspect ratio - Stretches the desktop as much as possible to fit the display while maintaining the aspect ratio. If the desktop has a different aspect ratio than the display, there may be black bands around the desktop.
  • Full-screen - Stretches the desktop to fit the entire display screen, even if the aspect ratio is not the same.
  • No scaling - The display image remains the original size and is centered on your display screen. This may result in a small but crisp image. The remaining area around the image is surrounded by black bars.
  • Integer scaling - Stretches the desktop as much as possible in an integer factor such as x1, x2, x3 etc to fit the display while maintaining the aspect ratio. If the desktop has a different aspect ratio than the display, there may be blank borders around the desktop.
scalingModeInfo.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Aspect ratio - The shape of the desktop looks distorted.
  • Full-screen - Make the desktop image as large as possible on your display.
  • No scaling - Your desktop appears blurry when scaled.
  • Integer scaling - The desktop looks blurry at low resolution.

performScalingOnInfo.description: Select where you want the scaling to be performed.
performScalingOnInfo.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Select the GPU if you want the graphics card to do all the scaling.
  • Select the display if you want the display to scale the image generated by the GPU.

scalingPreviewInfo.description: Select a resolution and refresh rate for previewing the scaling settings.

video.title: Video
video.colorSettingsDescription: Adjust settings for the selected display
video.rtxVideoEnhancementsDescriptionBoth: Adjust Super Resolution and HDR settings across all displays for applicable apps
video.rtxVideoEnhancementsDescriptionVsr: Adjust Super Resolution settings across all displays for applicable apps
video.rtxVideoEnhancementsDescriptionHdr: Adjust HDR settings across all displays for applicable apps

vsrInfoPopup.infoUpdateDriver: Some Super Resolution features may require updating your drivers to the latest version.
vsrInfoPopup.description: Uses AI to upscale resolution and reduce compression artifacts on videos played in supported browsers and apps. Status indicates if Super Resolution is active and the quality level currently applied.
vsrInfoPopup.typicalUsageScenarios: Full screen video playback on 1440p or higher resolution panels.
vsrInfoPopup.genericTips:
• This feature will scale video from its original size to your playback window size.
  • The effect is applied on the first video that you play.
vsrInfoPopup.hybridSystemTips:
• This feature will scale video from its original size to your playback window size.
  • The effect is applied on the first video that you play.
  • To activate RTX Video on your supported browser, go to the Windows control panel. Under System -> Display -> Graphics -> Custom options for apps, set your browser to run in “High Performance Mode”.
    Warning: Will impact battery life when browser is open.

hdrInfoPopup.infoUpdateDriver: Some HDR features may require updating your drivers to the latest version.
hdrInfoPopup.description: Uses AI to convert SDR color space video to HDR. Requires HDR compatible panel and HDR enabled in Windows Display Settings.
hdrInfoPopup.typicalUsageScenarios: Playing traditional video on an HDR10 compatible display with improved color depth and detail.
hdrInfoPopup.errorWindowsSetting: HDR is disabled. Enable it from Windows Display Settings.
hdrInfoPopup.genericTips: • The effect is applied on the first video that you play.
hdrInfoPopup.hybridSystemTips:
• The effect is applied on the first video that you play.
  • To activate RTX Video on your supported browser, go to the Windows control panel. Under System -> Display -> Graphics -> Custom options for apps, set your browser to run in “High Performance Mode”.
    Warning: Will impact battery life when browser is open.

videoColorModeInfoPopup.description: Select “Video Player” to use your video player’s color adjustment controls. Select “NVIDIA” to use the color settings specified in the NVIDIA App.
videoColorModeInfoPopup.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Video Player - You are satisfied with the color controls in your movie player application and want different color settings for different applications.
  • NVIDIA - You are not satisfied with the color controls in your movie player applications and want the same color settings for all your video applications.

videoColorSettingInfoPopup.description:
• Brightness - Adjusts the lightness or darkness of your video.
  • Contrast - Adjusts the difference between the lightest and darkest parts of the video image.
  • Hue - Adjusts the hue of your image.
  • Saturation - Adjusts the color level of your video image.
videoColorSettingInfoPopup.typicalUsageScenarios:
• Brightness - Use when the video image is too light or dark.
  • Contrast - Use when the video image appears gray or washed out.
  • Hue - Use when the video image is too green/yellow or too red/pink.
  • Saturation - Use when the color level of your video image is too strong or weak.

videoColorDynamicRangeInfoPopup.description: Dynamic range allows the user to select the dynamic range (16-235 or 0-255) of the video content to preserve black detail and white peaks based on the type of content being viewed.
videoColorDynamicRangeInfoPopup.typicalUsageScenarios:
• The “Limited (16-235)” setting is commonly used by many televisions.
  • The “Full (0-255)” setting may allow for more detail in the dark and white areas of some content.

video.hdrDialogWindowsErrorString: HDR is disabled from Windows Display Settings.
video.videoRestoreDialogText: Restore video settings to factory defaults?

applyRevertDialog.title: Keep Changes?
applyRevertDialog.description:
Your desktop configuration has changed.
Would you like to keep these changes?

performance.title: Performance

autoTune.title: Automatic Tuning
autoTune.desc: Automatic tuner which finds the best overclock settings for your GPU and maintains that performance on a regular basis
autoTune.info:
Description
Automatic tuner which finds the best overclock settings using an advanced scanning algorithm, and manages your GPU tuning profile for you.
It's performed on a monthly basis when your system is idle to maintain optimal performance.
autoTune.interruptedByUserText: Automatic tuning was interrupted by user.
autoTune.interruptedByGpuText: Automatic tuning was interrupted due to high GPU utilization.
autoTune.interruptedByError: Automatic tuning was interrupted due to an error.
autoTune.laptopNotPluggedInError: Automatic tuning was interrupted as laptop was unplugged.
autoTune.dcModeMessage: Automatic tuning available when laptop is plugged in
autoTune.learnMoreMessageDc:
• {{interruptedText}}
  • Automatic tuning available when laptop is plugged in.
autoTune.learnMoreMessage:
• {{interruptedText}}
  • Follow up scan will be scheduled when system is idle.
  • If you want to tune now, you can still do so.
autoTune.restoreMessage: Your GPU will be restored back to the factory tuned state.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.message: Warning: Tuning your GPU or changing Performance Limits may cause your PC to show visual artifacts. Your PC may become unstable.
disclaimer.perfLimitNotSupportedMessage: Warning: Tuning your GPU may cause your PC to show visual artifacts. Your PC may become unstable.

performance.scanningMessage: Avoid GPU intensive tasks like gaming, video playback, video recording etc
performance.backgroundOcUnSupported: Automatic Tuning and Performance Limits are supported on GeForce GTX 16 series and RTX 20 series or greater GPUs
performance.backgroundOcUnSupportedArch64: Automatic Tuning and Performance Limits are supported on compatible NVIDIA GPUs.
performance.autoTuningDriverNotSupported: Automatic tuning is not supported on your current GPU driver. Please update to the latest driver and try again.
performance.autoTuningMultiGpuNotSupported: Automatic Tuning and Performance Limits are not supported currently for Multi-GPU systems

perfLimiters.title: Performance Limits
perfLimiters.info:
Description
Performance limits set the operating range for the automatic tuner to find the best overclock settings for your GPU.

crashNotification.title: Send Error and Crash Data
crashNotification.note: NVIDIA software has encountered an issue. Send detailed error logs and driver data that help NVIDIA fix bugs and improve drivers.
crashNotification.consent: By accepting, you agree to automatically send this and all future error logs. You can change this any time to going to NVIDIA App Privacy settings.

dlssOverrides.modelsRecommendedText: * Recommended by NVIDIA for optimal image quality and/or performance

error.graphicsApiMultipleErrorMessage: {{supportedApiFirst}} or {{supportedApiLast}} is required to use this feature

dlssOverrides.compatibilityMessageSupportFound: To use DLSS overrides, enable corresponding DLSS settings from the game’s settings menu
dlssOverrides.compatibilityMessageSupportNotFound:
This setting is supported for select DLSS games. To check for support:
  • Launch game and enable DLSS from the game’s settings menu
  • Check support status in NVIDIA Overlay > Statistics > DLSS
dlssOverrides.compatibilityMessageCommon: Check override status in NVIDIA Overlay > Statistics > DLSS
dlssOverrides.commonNotes: When overrides are active, in-game DLSS setting and optimisation will be overridden
dlssOverrides.commonCompatibleMessage: Available for compatible DLSS games and apps

multipleDisplays.title: Multiple displays

manageDisplays.title: Manage Displays
manageDisplays.unableToDeactiveTheDisplay: Unable to deactivate Surround Display from here

multipleDisplays.confirmationText: Changes have been made that must be applied before leaving this page. Would you like to apply these changes now?

surround.title: Surround
surround.changeDisplayDialogDescription: Changing the display configuration requires disabling Surround. Do you want to continue?
surround.disableSurroundDialogDescription: Are you sure you want to disable Surround?
surround.changeOrientationDialogDescription: Changing the display orientation will remove any changes to bezel correction and the resolution list. Do you want to continue?
surround.changeTopologyDialogDescription: Changing the topology requires disabling Surround. Do you want to continue?
surround.surroundDialogDescription: Surround is a feature that allows you to combine two or more separate displays to create a single synchronized visual canvas.
surround.surroundDialogTypicalUsageScenario:
• Full screen gaming
  • Watching fullscreen video
surround.surroundInfoDialogDiscription: Surround requires displays that share at least one common timing. Use monitors of the same make and size to get the best performance from NVIDIA Surround.
surround.surroundInfoDialogImportantInfo:
• In a multi-GPU Surround setup, make sure to connect the displays on same ports across GPUs.
  • In multi-GPU Surround, make sure to use the same output protocol for the displays across GPUs.
  • Make sure to have a stable display-to-GPU connection. Check cables or display docking stations. Check if extended desktop topology works as expected among the Surround candidate monitors.
surround.surroundShortcutsDescription: Use keyboard shortcuts to toggle the features
